name: Pibeste peak from Lourdes
name_original: Le pic du Pibeste depuis Lourdes
description:
This generous 23 kilometer loop covers several peaks around Lourdes which offer their share of panoramas over the heart of the Pyrenees chain. Pretty mountain trails are responsible for making the link between each of them.

World famous for its historical religious heritage, Lourdes attracts many pilgrims every year who come to meditate there. The city is placed at the gates of the Gaves valley. It extends from Lourdes at 325 meters above sea level, to Vignemale culminating at the Spanish border at 3,298 meters.

The departure is located at the Lourdes train station which is on the Piedmont line of the Occitanie Rail Tour. You join the Gave de Pau where the GR 101 path crosses, you will follow the markings to the village of Ossen. Take advantage of your crossing of the city to find your lunch. Indeed, when leaving Lourdes, you will find nothing on the way to refuel. Also bring enough water.

From the village, you embark on a climb that leads to the peak of Alian, the first belvedere of your journey at 1,089 meters above sea level. The ascent continues towards the Pic du Pibeste via a pretty ridge passage. Arrived at the top, the panorama on the chain of the Pyrenees seems infinite. In clear weather it is possible to observe a significant number of peaks.

The descent begins by sliding between the soum of Mont Agut and the mall of Arréou before reaching the village of Ségus. From there you undertake a new and final climb to Béout. This summit offers a beautiful view of the city of Lourdes that you are about to find. Back in the city, take a short detour through the area of the sanctuary of Lourdes before returning to the station.
region_intro: Hiking around Lourdes offers diverse landscapes at the foot of the Pyrenees in the Hautes-Pyrénées department of France. The region features a transition zone of Pyrenean foothills, prominent peaks like Pic du Jer and Pic du Pibeste, and tranquil glacial lakes such as Lac de Lourdes. River valleys, including the Gave de Pau, and extensive forests like the Bois de Lourdes, characterize the terrain, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in Lourdes The most popular hiking route is Loop of Lourdes, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the town and its surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Petit Jer – Pic du Jer loop from Lourdes, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail ascends to Pic du Jer, providing panoramic views of Lourdes and the Pyrenees. Local hikers also love the View of Lake Lourdes – Panoramic view of Lake Lourdes loop from Poueyferré, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through tranquil lakeside scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Hiking around Lourdes is defined by Pyrenean foothills, glacial lakes, and prominent peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 275 reviews. More than 2,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Lourdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lourdes?
A: There are over 70 hiking trails around Lourdes, offering a wide range of options from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents. The region's diverse landscapes, from Pyrenean foothills to prominent peaks and tranquil lakes, provide varied environments for outdoor activities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Lourdes?
A: The hiking routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 275 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, stunning panoramic views, and well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike in Lourdes?
A: Lourdes offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for most trails. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude routes in the Pyrenees. Winter hiking is possible, particularly on lower elevation paths, but requires appropriate gear and checking weather conditions for snow and ice.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Lourdes?
A: Hikes around Lourdes feature a diverse terrain, ranging from the gentle slopes of the Pyrenean foothills to the more rugged ascents of peaks like Pic du Jer and Pic du Pibeste. You'll find tranquil lakeside paths, forest trails within the Bois de Lourdes, and converted railway lines like the Gaves Greenway, offering a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or shorter walks?
A: Yes, Lourdes offers numerous easy trails perfect for beginners or those seeking shorter, more leisurely walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15013781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Lourdes – Panoramic view of Lake Lourdes loop from Poueyferré&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6-mile (5.9 km) trail around the tranquil Lac de Lourdes, often completed in under two hours. The area around Lourdes Lake also provides several other pleasant walks.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Lourdes?
A: Yes, many routes around Lourdes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871767354/loop-of-lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.2-mile (8.3 km) trail offering views of the town and surrounding hills. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28734314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Petit Jer – Pic du Jer loop from Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to Pic du Jer for panoramic vistas.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover on hikes near Lourdes?
A: The region boasts several impressive natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can hike to the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic du Jer&lt;/a&gt;, accessible by funicular or trail, for panoramic views of Lourdes and the Pyrenees. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pibeste Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/764243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the Summit of Béout&lt;/a&gt; also offer extensive vistas. The tranquil Lourdes Lake (Lac de Lourdes) is a beautiful glacial lake with a unique peat bog, a listed Natura 2000 site.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Lourdes?
A: Absolutely. Many trails around Lourdes are suitable for families. The paths around Lourdes Lake are generally flat and easy, perfect for walks with children. The Bois de Lourdes also offers numerous trails, picnic areas, and sports routes that are great for family outings. The funicular to Pic du Jer makes the summit accessible for all ages, with a botanical trail near the top.

Q: Can I bring my dog on hiking trails around Lourdes?
A: Many hiking trails in the Lourdes area are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock, and to respect local regulations regarding pet access. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

Q: How accessible are the hiking trails by public transport or car in Lourdes?
A: Lourdes is well-connected, making many trailheads accessible. The town center itself is a starting point for several routes, including the ascent to Pic du Jer, which is also served by a funicular. For trails further afield, driving is often the most convenient option, with parking available near popular trailheads and attractions like Lourdes Lake. Some long-distance routes like the GR 101 and GR 78 pass directly through Lourdes, making them accessible via public transport connections to the town.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Lourdes?
A: Yes, you'll find various options for refreshments. In Lourdes town, there are numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops. Near popular spots like Lourdes Lake, you might find picnic areas or small eateries. For trails leading to summits like Pic du Jer, there are facilities at the top. For longer hikes, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when venturing into more remote areas.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in the Lourdes area?
A: Generally, you do not need specific permits for day hiking on the established trails around Lourdes. However, if you plan to enter certain protected natural reserves or engage in specific activities, it's always wise to check with local tourist information or park authorities for any specific regulations or requirements. For general hiking, the trails are freely accessible.

Q: Beyond hiking, what other attractions are there to explore in Lourdes and its surroundings?
A: Lourdes offers a rich array of attractions beyond its hiking trails. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, a major pilgrimage site. Explore the historic Fortified Castle of Lourdes, or venture to the impressive &lt;a href="https://en.lourdes-infotourisme.com/explore/enjoy-a-wealth-of-outdoor-activities/the-most-stunning-natural-sites-at-the-gates-of-lourdes/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grottes de Bétharram&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular underground cave system located a short drive away. The region also serves as a gateway to other renowned Pyrenean sites like the Cirque de Gavarnie and Pont d'Espagne.
tips:
Lourdes is world famous for its chapel of Our Lady of Lourdes. Thousands of pilgrims go there every year.

The city is mainly built around its religious heritage. However, it is located at the foot of some beautiful peaks which offer lovely views of the Pyrenees chain. Lourdes is also crossed by the GR 101 trail.

The peak of Alian culminates at 1,089 meters on the heights of the city of Lourdes.
The passage of the breach of the same name provides access to Pibeste (1,349 meters). Its summit offers a nice panorama and a beautiful view of the valley.

The peak of Pibeste culminates at 1,349 meters above sea level on the heights of the city. Its location allows to open a nice panorama on the chain of the Pyrenees as well as the valley which is below. A few meters from the top is an old luxury hotel.

Culminating at 792 meters above sea level, Béout is an easily accessible summit in the Batsurguère valley. It forms a pretty natural belvedere which offers a beautiful panorama of the city of Lourdes.

The grotto of Lourdes is one of the places of the greatest importance for devout Christians.

Since the apparitions of the Virgin in the time of Marie-Bernadette Soubirous, the place has changed a lot. It has been laid out in such a way that it can accommodate a crowd of pilgrims, which are still numerous year after year. On both sides of the Gave de Pau, there are many monuments.

In addition to the Grotto of Massabelle, which with the water from the Grotto and the swimming pools represents a bit of the "holy of holies", the imposing church of Sainte-Bernadette is impressive.

The route also takes you to the Eucharist adoration tent, the Pieta, the adoration chapel and the so-called Matter Dolorosa.

waypoints.name: View from the Summit of Béout
